questions.question: With reference to computing, what does ASCII stand for?
questions.option_1: Algorithmic Standard Code for International Information
questions.option_2: American Systematic Code for International Interoperability
questions.option_3: American Standard Code for Information Interchange
questions.option_4: American Standard Code for Information Integrity

questions.question: With reference to computer systems, what does CPU stand for?
questions.option_1: Computer Processing Utility
questions.option_2: Central Programming Unit
questions.option_3: Control Processing Unit
questions.option_4: Central Processing Unit

questions.question: In the architecture of a Central Processing Unit (CPU), which statement accurately distinguishes the function of the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) from that of the Control Unit (CU)?
questions.option_1: CU executes arithmetic operations, and ALU controls program instructions.
questions.option_2: ALU handles calculations and logic functions; CU manages instruction flow and coordination.
questions.option_3: CU interacts directly with peripheral devices, and ALU stores temporary data.
questions.option_4: ALU decodes instructions, while CU handles data storage tasks.

questions.question: How many unique characters can be represented using the standard ASCII code?
Which of the following numbers is used to represent the ASCII value of the uppercase letter 'B'?
CMOS, often associated with storing system configuration, stands for:
questions.option_1: Computerised Main Operating System
questions.option_2: Complementary Metal-Oxide Semiconductor
questions.option_3: Configurable Modular Operating Software
questions.option_4: Central Memory Output System

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding ASCII, LAN, and CD in computer terminology?
questions.option_1: LAN refers to a wide geographical network like the internet.
questions.option_2: ASCII represents characters using numeric codes in binary.
questions.option_3: ASCII is used for formatting disks in a LAN network.
questions.option_4: CD is a rewritable magnetic storage medium.

questions.question: Considering the fundamental operations of a computer, what is the immediate effect of pressing the Tab key within a command-line interface (CLI) or terminal?
questions.option_1: It deletes the last typed character.
questions.option_2: It attempts to auto-complete the currently typed command or filename.
questions.option_3: It executes the currently typed command.
questions.option_4: It switches to a different virtual terminal or console.

questions.question: What is the full form of CD, commonly used in storage devices?
questions.option_1: Computer Disk
questions.option_2: Compact Disc
questions.option_3: Circuit Drive
questions.option_4: Compact Data

questions.question: Which option correctly explains the fundamental difference between input and output devices in a computer system?
questions.option_1: Input devices run pre-loaded instructions from memory, whereas output devices collect environmental data.
questions.option_2: Input devices change user inputs into digital format, while output devices permanently save system data.
questions.option_3: Input devices enable users to send data into the system, while output devices communicate processed information back to the user.
questions.option_4: Input devices directly interact with the CPU, while output devices work independently without CPU involvement.

questions.question: Which command in Windows 10 CMD will delete all .log files in the current directory without asking for confirmation?
questions.option_1: del *.log /q
questions.option_2: del *.log
questions.option_3: del *.log /p
questions.option_4: erase *.log

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of virtual memory in a computer system?
questions.option_1: To extend CACHE memory for storage
questions.option_2: To provide backup for data
questions.option_3: To extend RAM capacity by using disk space
questions.option_4: To increase physical storage capacity

questions.question: What is the main purpose of cache memory in a computer?
questions.option_1: To store frequently accessed data for quick retrieval
questions.option_2: To serve as virtual memory
questions.option_3: To provide backup for RAM
questions.option_4: To permanently store user files

questions.question: What is the primary function of the CPU in a computer system?
questions.option_1: Store data permanently
questions.option_2: Connect input and output devices
questions.option_3: Execute instructions and process data
questions.option_4: Manage the power supply

questions.question: Which of the following best encapsulates the fundamental difference between the von Neumann and Harvard architectures in computer systems?
questions.option_1: The von Neumann architecture is primarily used in modern microcontrollers, while the Harvard architecture is prevalent in general-purpose computers.
questions.option_2: The von Neumann architecture utilises separate address spaces for instructions and data, while the Harvard architecture uses a single address space.
questions.option_3: The Harvard architecture allows simultaneous fetching of instructions and data, potentially improving performance, while the von Neumann architecture typically fetches them sequentially from the same memory space.
questions.option_4: The Harvard architecture employs a more complex control unit compared to the von Neumann architecture.

questions.question: What is the significance of pipelining in CPU architecture?
questions.option_1: Executes multiple instructions simultaneously by overlapping stages
questions.option_2: Stores instructions in memory
questions.option_3: Transfers data between registers
questions.option_4: Increases clock speed using multiple cores

questions.question: Which of the following best encapsulates the concept of a 'stored-program computer' as introduced by the Von Neumann architecture?
questions.option_1: A computer where both data and instructions are stored in the same primary memory for sequential execution
questions.option_2: A computer that utilises separate memory units for data and instructions to optimise processing speed
questions.option_3: A computer that relies primarily on external storage devices for program execution
questions.option_4: A computer that can only execute a fixed set of instructions hardwired into its circuitry

questions.question: What is the fundamental difference between a 'cold boot' and a 'warm boot' of a computer system?
questions.option_1: A cold boot preserves the current state of applications, while a warm boot closes them.
questions.option_2: A cold boot only reloads the kernel, while a warm boot reinitializes all hardware components.
questions.option_3: A cold boot restarts the operating system from a powered-off state, while a warm boot restarts it from a running state.
questions.option_4: A cold boot is initiated by the user, while a warm boot is initiated by the operating system due to an error.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the American Standard Code for Information Interchange (ASCII)?
questions.option_1: To enable secure encryption of data for internet transmission
questions.option_2: To design a graphical user interface for operating systems
questions.option_3: To define a standardised set of codes for representing text and control characters
questions.option_4: To create a programming language for text-based applications

questions.question: What is the purpose of pressing Alt + Tab on the keyboard of a computer system?
questions.option_1: Minimises all windows
questions.option_2: Opens Task Manager
questions.option_3: Shuts down the system
questions.option_4: Switches between open applications

questions.question: What is the purpose of creating subfolders when organizing files?
questions.option_1: To reduce the file size
questions.option_2: To automatically back up files to the cloud
questions.option_3: To group related files together for better management
questions.option_4: To change file extensions

questions.question: What is the primary function of Alt + Tab in most modern operating systems?
questions.option_1: Switches between open applications
questions.option_2: Opens a new tab in the current application
questions.option_3: Opens Task Manager directly
questions.option_4: Minimises the active window

questions.question: What is a good way to keep your files organized on a computer?
questions.option_1: Remove folders regularly
questions.option_2: Change file names every day
questions.option_3: Store everything in a single folder
questions.option_4: Use separate folders for different types of files

questions.question: What is the significance of the 'root directory' in a hierarchical file system structure?
questions.option_1: It is a special directory that contains essential operating system kernel files.
questions.option_2: It is a temporary directory used by the operating system for caching frequently accessed files.
questions.option_3: It is the topmost directory in the file system hierarchy, from which all other directories branch.
questions.option_4: It is the directory where all user-specific files are stored.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the Tab key on a standard keyboard?
questions.option_1: To insert a forward slash
questions.option_2: To close the active application
questions.option_3: To shift the cursor to the next field or tab position
questions.option_4: To remove text
